Multimodal image fusion in remote sensing, integrating data from sources like SAR, optical, multispectral, and hyperspectral sensors, is critical for applications such as land cover classification and disaster monitoring. However, challenges like modality heterogeneity, cloud occlusion, and cross-domain variability demand advanced fusion algorithms capable of capturing nonlinear correlations and ensuring robust generalization.We propose MAFFUNet, a lightweight U-Net-based framework with a five-layer encoder-decoder architecture, incorporating depthwise separable convolutions for efficiency and temporal data support via flattening. MAFFUNet integrates three novel modules: Adversarial Feature Fusion (AFF) for maximizing mutual information, Hybrid Attention and Multiscale Fusion (HAMF) for optimizing spectral and spatial features, and Cross-Domain Regularization (CDR) for enhancing cross-domain adaptability.Experiments on the Chikusei, PaviaC, and PaviaU datasets at 4 times times , 8 times times , and 16 times times scaling factors demonstrate that MAFFUNet outperforms traditional methods and deep learning approaches in metrics like RMSE, PSNR, ERGAS, and SAM, with superior spectral fidelity and spatial detail preservation. Its low computational complexity suits resource-constrained environments.MAFFUNet provides an efficient and robust solution for multimodal remote sensing image fusion.

item.title: RSTFA: Efficient Training-Free Human Preference Alignment via Rejection Sampling for Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
item.dc:creator: Hongzheng Yang
Jason Chun-Lok Li
Li Kun
Wenao Ma
Mingjie Xu
Yuzhi Zhao
Lai-Man Po
item.description: Given a text-to-image diffusion model pretrained on large-scale text-image pairs, can we align the model with human preferences without further fine-tuning? In this paper, we analyze the effect of alignment tuning in diffusion models by comparing the diffusion denoising trajectory between base and aligned models. Our findings reveal that alignment tuning primarily affects superficial stylistic aspects during denoising, rather than fundamental content, suggesting superficial alignment behaviors. Based on this discovery, we introduce a novel, training-free alignment approach (RSTFA) that leverages rejection sampling at specific stylistic timesteps, ensuring human preference alignment without fine-tuning or heavy inference overhead. We provide a theoretical analysis and derive a bias bound for our rejection-sampling alignment scheme. Empirically, we show that RSTFA better preserves sample diversity than reinforcement-learning-based tuning methods. Extensive experiments on Pick-a-Pic, COCO, HPD V2, and PartiPrompts show that our method not only achieves superior alignment with human preferences compared to state-of-the-art methods, but also reduces computational demands, establishing efficient, human-centered diffusion model alignment. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:35+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.774 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hongzheng Yang; Jason Chun-Lok Li; Li Kun; Wenao Ma; Mingjie Xu; Yuzhi Zhao; Lai-Man Po

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3684401

Relevance: 0.774 (must_read)

Given a text-to-image diffusion model pretrained on large-scale text-image pairs, can we align the model with human preferences without further fine-tuning? In this paper, we analyze the effect of alignment tuning in diffusion models by comparing the diffusion denoising trajectory between base and aligned models. Our findings reveal that alignment tuning primarily affects superficial stylistic aspects during denoising, rather than fundamental content, suggesting superficial alignment behaviors. Based on this discovery, we introduce a novel, training-free alignment approach (RSTFA) that leverages rejection sampling at specific stylistic timesteps, ensuring human preference alignment without fine-tuning or heavy inference overhead. We provide a theoretical analysis and derive a bias bound for our rejection-sampling alignment scheme. Empirically, we show that RSTFA better preserves sample diversity than reinforcement-learning-based tuning methods. Extensive experiments on Pick-a-Pic, COCO, HPD V2, and PartiPrompts show that our method not only achieves superior alignment with human preferences compared to state-of-the-art methods, but also reduces computational demands, establishing efficient, human-centered diffusion model alignment.

item.title: FSMD–Net: Joint Spatial–Channel Spectral Modeling for SAR Ship Detection in Complex Inshore Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Xianxun Yao
Yijiang Shen
Yuheng Lei
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship detection in complex inshore scenarios has long been constrained by the coupled effects of speckle noise and small–scale weak scattering targets. Although feature–level frequency–domain denoising methods partially alleviate noise interference, existing studies predominantly focus on spatial frequency modeling and implicitly assume consistent spectral responses and discriminative contributions across channels. This assumption may lead to over–suppression of weak ship targets under complex backgrounds. To address the incomplete dimensionality of current frequency–domain modeling, this paper proposes FSMD–Net, a joint spatial–channel spectral modeling framework for SAR ship detection. During multi–scale feature fusion, a coordinated modulation mechanism integrating multi–spectral channel attention with spatial frequency–domain denoising is introduced. This design enables channel discriminability and frequency–subspace denoising to act synergistically, enforcing structurally consistent spectral constraints throughout multi–scale feature propagation. Extensive experiments on SARDet–100K, HRSID, and AIR–SARShip–2.0 demonstrate that FSMD–Net achieves consistent performance improvements, particularly in small–target and strong–clutter scenarios, exhibiting enhanced detection accuracy and robustness. Published: 2026-04-21T16:10:44+00:00 Venue: Remote Sensing Score: 0.774 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xianxun Yao; Yijiang Shen; Yuheng Lei

Journal: Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.3390/rs18081254

Relevance: 0.774 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship detection in complex inshore scenarios has long been constrained by the coupled effects of speckle noise and small–scale weak scattering targets. Although feature–level frequency–domain denoising methods partially alleviate noise interference, existing studies predominantly focus on spatial frequency modeling and implicitly assume consistent spectral responses and discriminative contributions across channels. This assumption may lead to over–suppression of weak ship targets under complex backgrounds. To address the incomplete dimensionality of current frequency–domain modeling, this paper proposes FSMD–Net, a joint spatial–channel spectral modeling framework for SAR ship detection. During multi–scale feature fusion, a coordinated modulation mechanism integrating multi–spectral channel attention with spatial frequency–domain denoising is introduced. This design enables channel discriminability and frequency–subspace denoising to act synergistically, enforcing structurally consistent spectral constraints throughout multi–scale feature propagation. Extensive experiments on SARDet–100K, HRSID, and AIR–SARShip–2.0 demonstrate that FSMD–Net achieves consistent performance improvements, particularly in small–target and strong–clutter scenarios, exhibiting enhanced detection accuracy and robustness.

item.title: Energy Diffusion Prior for Infrared Small Target Detection: Build Representations for Objects instead of Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Yunfei Tong
Hai Yang
Ganghao Liu
Zhe Wang
item.description: Infrared small target detection aims to recognize and locate distant targets from thermal images obtained by infrared imaging systems. The small proportion of distant targets in the image makes it a challenging task to achieve accurate and effective target representation. In this paper, we propose an efficient detection model called DPNet based on the principle of infrared imaging. This model provides a more effective representation for small targets through feature recovery and curvature separation. Specifically, the feature recovery network retains the attention of small targets in deep networks by utilizing a simple encoding space and a multi-stage decoding space, reducing redundant skip connections. The curvature separation module leverages the thermal radiation diffusion properties of small targets as prior knowledge and extracts key target features through a learnable discrete Fourier transform. By reducing redundancy and incorporating prior knowledge, our network can learn effective target features for the detection task more efficiently and purposefully, rather than relying on complex networks to learn the variable backgrounds in different scenes. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method is more efficient and accurate than state-of-the-art methods. Code is available at https://github.com/tarayatong/EnergyDiffusionPrior . Published: 2026-04-21T06:59:35+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.773 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yunfei Tong; Hai Yang; Ganghao Liu; Zhe Wang

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132503

Relevance: 0.773 (must_read)

Infrared small target detection aims to recognize and locate distant targets from thermal images obtained by infrared imaging systems. The small proportion of distant targets in the image makes it a challenging task to achieve accurate and effective target representation. In this paper, we propose an efficient detection model called DPNet based on the principle of infrared imaging. This model provides a more effective representation for small targets through feature recovery and curvature separation. Specifically, the feature recovery network retains the attention of small targets in deep networks by utilizing a simple encoding space and a multi-stage decoding space, reducing redundant skip connections. The curvature separation module leverages the thermal radiation diffusion properties of small targets as prior knowledge and extracts key target features through a learnable discrete Fourier transform. By reducing redundancy and incorporating prior knowledge, our network can learn effective target features for the detection task more efficiently and purposefully, rather than relying on complex networks to learn the variable backgrounds in different scenes. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method is more efficient and accurate than state-of-the-art methods. Code is available at https://github.com/tarayatong/EnergyDiffusionPrior .

item.title: Adv-STMamba: A Lightweight Spatial-Temporal Mamba Model Incorporating Adversarial Training for Radar HRRPTarget Recognition
item.dc:creator: Ke Wang
Xiaoyi Pan
Xiaofeng Ai
Zhiming Xu
Feng Zhao
Shunping Xiao
Siwei Chen
Yafei Song
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
item.description: The electromagnetic space of modern battlefields is becoming increasingly complex. High-Resolution Range Profile (HRRP) has emerged as a core data carrier in the field of Radar Automatic Target Recognition (RATR), due to its advantages of easy acquisition, low storage resource requirements, and high processing efficiency. However, the complex electromagnetic environment inten sifies HRRP's sensitivity to noise and target attitude changes. Existing HRRP recognition methods generally suffer from weak feature extraction capabilities, poor dynamic adaptability, and insufficient robustness in complex environments. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes an Adv-STMamba model, which is built upon a Spatial-Temporal Mamba module (STMamba) and integrates with to dedicated modules: Adversarial Label Smoothing and Adaptive Attention Gate modules. Specifically, Adversarial Label Smoothing resolves the problem of model's overconfidence in labels by generating adversarial soft labels that maximize model loss; STMamba enables in-depth mining of spatiotemporal features in HRRP sequences; Adaptive Attention Gate module achieves efficient spatiotemporal feature fusion by adaptively assigning feature weights, thereby enhancing model's robustness against attitude disturbances. To ensure the persuasiveness of experimental data, this paper converts the public MSTAR dataset into an HRRP dataset and conducts verification through comparative experiments, robustness experiments, complexity experiments, and ablation experiments. The experimental results show that Adv-STMamba model can effectively extract deep-seated features from HRRP data under different signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) scenarios. It achieves a recognition accuracy of 99.67% under the high SNR scenario of 30dB and maintains a recognition accuracy of 97.30% even under the low SNR scenario of 0dB. This fully verifies the effectiveness of Adv-STMamba model in HRRP recognition tasks under complex environments. Published: 2026-04-21T19:51:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems Score: 0.773 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Ke Wang; Xiaoyi Pan; Xiaofeng Ai; Zhiming Xu; Feng Zhao; Shunping Xiao; Siwei Chen; Yafei Song

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems

DOI: 10.1109/taes.2026.3685957

Relevance: 0.773 (must_read)

The electromagnetic space of modern battlefields is becoming increasingly complex. High-Resolution Range Profile (HRRP) has emerged as a core data carrier in the field of Radar Automatic Target Recognition (RATR), due to its advantages of easy acquisition, low storage resource requirements, and high processing efficiency. However, the complex electromagnetic environment inten sifies HRRP's sensitivity to noise and target attitude changes. Existing HRRP recognition methods generally suffer from weak feature extraction capabilities, poor dynamic adaptability, and insufficient robustness in complex environments. To address the aforementioned issues, this paper proposes an Adv-STMamba model, which is built upon a Spatial-Temporal Mamba module (STMamba) and integrates with to dedicated modules: Adversarial Label Smoothing and Adaptive Attention Gate modules. Specifically, Adversarial Label Smoothing resolves the problem of model's overconfidence in labels by generating adversarial soft labels that maximize model loss; STMamba enables in-depth mining of spatiotemporal features in HRRP sequences; Adaptive Attention Gate module achieves efficient spatiotemporal feature fusion by adaptively assigning feature weights, thereby enhancing model's robustness against attitude disturbances. To ensure the persuasiveness of experimental data, this paper converts the public MSTAR dataset into an HRRP dataset and conducts verification through comparative experiments, robustness experiments, complexity experiments, and ablation experiments. The experimental results show that Adv-STMamba model can effectively extract deep-seated features from HRRP data under different signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) scenarios. It achieves a recognition accuracy of 99.67% under the high SNR scenario of 30dB and maintains a recognition accuracy of 97.30% even under the low SNR scenario of 0dB. This fully verifies the effectiveness of Adv-STMamba model in HRRP recognition tasks under complex environments.

item.title: Efficient spatial pyramid for object detection
item.dc:creator: Banu Wirawan Yohanes
Philip O. Ogunbona
Wanqing Li
item.description: One-stage object detectors emerge as a trade-off between detection accuracy and speed. However, they do not exploit long-range context relationship and their performance can easily drop in complex scenes. In this work, we propose efficient spatial pyramid based on graph model of Laplacian with a novel graph correlation filter. This filter is designed to measure symmetric uncertainty among features to learn long-range context relationship, while preserving important features. Furthermore, we employ Winograd algorithm to reduce floating point operations significantly without decreasing detection performance, by trading-off costly multiplication operations with more additions. They enable an efficient object detection. Extensive experiments were conducted on two challenging object detection datasets, COCO and KITTI. The proposed network was compared to state-of-the-art efficient object detectors, MobileNet-SSD Lite, YOLO, MobileVIT, Tiny DSOD, and EfficientDet. Detailed convergence proof and training epoch analysis provide strong support and evidence for the achieved results improving overall detection accuracy in complex scenes with less computational resources. Published: 2026-04-22T06:49:59+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.772 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Banu Wirawan Yohanes; Philip O. Ogunbona; Wanqing Li

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113702

Relevance: 0.772 (must_read)

One-stage object detectors emerge as a trade-off between detection accuracy and speed. However, they do not exploit long-range context relationship and their performance can easily drop in complex scenes. In this work, we propose efficient spatial pyramid based on graph model of Laplacian with a novel graph correlation filter. This filter is designed to measure symmetric uncertainty among features to learn long-range context relationship, while preserving important features. Furthermore, we employ Winograd algorithm to reduce floating point operations significantly without decreasing detection performance, by trading-off costly multiplication operations with more additions. They enable an efficient object detection. Extensive experiments were conducted on two challenging object detection datasets, COCO and KITTI. The proposed network was compared to state-of-the-art efficient object detectors, MobileNet-SSD Lite, YOLO, MobileVIT, Tiny DSOD, and EfficientDet. Detailed convergence proof and training epoch analysis provide strong support and evidence for the achieved results improving overall detection accuracy in complex scenes with less computational resources.

item.title: Motion–disturbance decoupling with interaction-aware association for satellite video multi-object tracking
item.dc:creator: Yuman Yuan
Tianyu Deng
Hongyang Bai
Shuai Guo
Yuanbo Chen
item.description: Multi-object tracking (MOT) in satellite video remains challenging because weak target responses, large static backgrounds, and frequent target interactions degrade both detection reliability and data association quality. To address these challenges, a motion-disturbance decoupling network, termed MI-ED, is proposed. The framework models target dynamics with two components: a predictable motion component and an interaction- or occlusion-induced disturbance component. This decoupling is then used to improve detection and association within a unified framework. On the detection side, a motion prior perception module integrates long-term, short-term, and reversed temporal cues, while a motion-guided cross-scale attention feature pyramid network enhances sparse dynamic responses and suppresses static background interference. On the tracking side, an environmental disturbance inference module estimates local interaction risk and recalibrates detection confidence, and a disturbance-aware multi-stage association strategy is used to preserve reliable weak detections and recover targets after short-term misses. Extensive experiments on AIR-MOT and VISO show that, relative to a strong competing method, MI-ED yields MOTA gains of 2.7 and 7.9 points while reducing FP/FN by 19.0%/6.2% and 13.6%/24.4%, respectively, demonstrating strong robustness in dense and occluded satellite scenarios. Published: 2026-04-21T16:06:38+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.771 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yuman Yuan; Tianyu Deng; Hongyang Bai; Shuai Guo; Yuanbo Chen

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116010

Relevance: 0.771 (must_read)

Multi-object tracking (MOT) in satellite video remains challenging because weak target responses, large static backgrounds, and frequent target interactions degrade both detection reliability and data association quality. To address these challenges, a motion-disturbance decoupling network, termed MI-ED, is proposed. The framework models target dynamics with two components: a predictable motion component and an interaction- or occlusion-induced disturbance component. This decoupling is then used to improve detection and association within a unified framework. On the detection side, a motion prior perception module integrates long-term, short-term, and reversed temporal cues, while a motion-guided cross-scale attention feature pyramid network enhances sparse dynamic responses and suppresses static background interference. On the tracking side, an environmental disturbance inference module estimates local interaction risk and recalibrates detection confidence, and a disturbance-aware multi-stage association strategy is used to preserve reliable weak detections and recover targets after short-term misses. Extensive experiments on AIR-MOT and VISO show that, relative to a strong competing method, MI-ED yields MOTA gains of 2.7 and 7.9 points while reducing FP/FN by 19.0%/6.2% and 13.6%/24.4%, respectively, demonstrating strong robustness in dense and occluded satellite scenarios.

item.title: Structure-Semantic Decoupled Modulation of Global Geospatial Embeddings for High-Resolution Remote Sensing Mapping
item.dc:creator: Jienan Lyu
Miao Yang
Jinchen Cai
Yiwen Hu
Guanyi Lu
Junhao Qiu
Runmin Dong
item.description: Fine-grained high-resolution remote sensing mapping typically relies on localized visual features, which restricts cross-domain generalizability and often leads to fragmented predictions of large-scale land covers. While global geospatial foundation models offer powerful, generalizable representations, directly fusing their high-dimensional implicit embeddings with high-resolution visual features frequently triggers feature interference and spatial structure degradation due to a severe semantic-spatial gap. To overcome these limitations, we propose a Structure-Semantic Decoupled Modulation (SSDM) framework, which decouples global geospatial representations into two complementary cross-modal injection pathways. First, the structural prior modulation branch introduces the macroscopic receptive field priors from global representations into the self-attention modules of the high-resolution encoder. By guiding local feature extraction with holistic structural constraints, it effectively suppresses prediction fragmentation caused by high-frequency detail noise and excessive intra-class variance. Second, the global semantic injection branch explicitly aligns holistic context with the deep high-resolution feature space and directly supplements global semantics via cross-modal integration, thereby significantly enhancing the semantic consistency and category-level discrimination of complex land covers. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing cross-modal fusion approaches. By unleashing the potential of global embeddings, SSDM consistently improves high-resolution mapping accuracy across diverse scenarios, providing a universal and effective paradigm for integrating geospatial foundation models into high-resolution vision tasks. Published: 2026-04-21T15:42:34+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.771 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jienan Lyu; Miao Yang; Jinchen Cai; Yiwen Hu; Guanyi Lu; Junhao Qiu; Runmin Dong

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.771 (must_read)

Fine-grained high-resolution remote sensing mapping typically relies on localized visual features, which restricts cross-domain generalizability and often leads to fragmented predictions of large-scale land covers. While global geospatial foundation models offer powerful, generalizable representations, directly fusing their high-dimensional implicit embeddings with high-resolution visual features frequently triggers feature interference and spatial structure degradation due to a severe semantic-spatial gap. To overcome these limitations, we propose a Structure-Semantic Decoupled Modulation (SSDM) framework, which decouples global geospatial representations into two complementary cross-modal injection pathways. First, the structural prior modulation branch introduces the macroscopic receptive field priors from global representations into the self-attention modules of the high-resolution encoder. By guiding local feature extraction with holistic structural constraints, it effectively suppresses prediction fragmentation caused by high-frequency detail noise and excessive intra-class variance. Second, the global semantic injection branch explicitly aligns holistic context with the deep high-resolution feature space and directly supplements global semantics via cross-modal integration, thereby significantly enhancing the semantic consistency and category-level discrimination of complex land covers. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to existing cross-modal fusion approaches. By unleashing the potential of global embeddings, SSDM consistently improves high-resolution mapping accuracy across diverse scenarios, providing a universal and effective paradigm for integrating geospatial foundation models into high-resolution vision tasks.

item.title: HEdge-MamYOLO: High Frequency Edge Information Facilitated Cross-Domain Feature Interaction Mamba for UAV Images Small Objects Detection
item.dc:creator: Chengjun Wang
Zhihui Cao
Jingyin Wang
Yonghua Jiang
Mingjun Deng
Lijing Bu
Guohui Deng
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) small object detection remains challenging due to extreme scale variation, dense spatial distribution, and frequent occlusion in aerial images. Small objects often occupy very limited pixel regions and exhibit ambiguous structural boundaries, making their discriminative features easily overwhelmed by background clutter. These challenges lead to unstable multi scale representation and degraded localization precision in existing detectors. To address these challenges, combining Mamba and YOLO, we propose the HEdge-MamYOLO, which consists of three components. First, we design Frequency-Mamba Collaborative High-Frequency Enhancement Module (FM-CHFEM) to extract high-frequency components of enhanced visible edges from occluded objects in the frequency domain and leverage Mamba global selective scanning combined with spatial similarity to unoccluded objects of the same type to achieve feature enhancement and recognition of occluded objects. Second, we propose the Dynamic Scale Feature Fusion Module (DSFFM), by covering large-scale objects and adaptively matching small-scale object features, achieves balanced capture of objects at different scales under FM-CHFEM-based feature extraction. Finally, we design the Lightweight Low-Level Feature Fusion Head (LLFFH) by integrating high-resolution low-level features with DSFFM’s scale-adaptive features, it sparsely processes feature maps via partial convolution. This approach preserves details of small objects, and reduces computational overhead. Extensive experiments on the VisDrone2019 and UAVDT aerial image datasets, with mAP50 metrics reaching 52.5%and 33.4% respectively, which is better than other SOTA models, demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method. Published: 2026-04-21T19:49:14+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.771 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Chengjun Wang; Zhihui Cao; Jingyin Wang; Yonghua Jiang; Mingjun Deng; Lijing Bu; Guohui Deng

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3686228

Relevance: 0.771 (must_read)

Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) small object detection remains challenging due to extreme scale variation, dense spatial distribution, and frequent occlusion in aerial images. Small objects often occupy very limited pixel regions and exhibit ambiguous structural boundaries, making their discriminative features easily overwhelmed by background clutter. These challenges lead to unstable multi scale representation and degraded localization precision in existing detectors. To address these challenges, combining Mamba and YOLO, we propose the HEdge-MamYOLO, which consists of three components. First, we design Frequency-Mamba Collaborative High-Frequency Enhancement Module (FM-CHFEM) to extract high-frequency components of enhanced visible edges from occluded objects in the frequency domain and leverage Mamba global selective scanning combined with spatial similarity to unoccluded objects of the same type to achieve feature enhancement and recognition of occluded objects. Second, we propose the Dynamic Scale Feature Fusion Module (DSFFM), by covering large-scale objects and adaptively matching small-scale object features, achieves balanced capture of objects at different scales under FM-CHFEM-based feature extraction. Finally, we design the Lightweight Low-Level Feature Fusion Head (LLFFH) by integrating high-resolution low-level features with DSFFM’s scale-adaptive features, it sparsely processes feature maps via partial convolution. This approach preserves details of small objects, and reduces computational overhead. Extensive experiments on the VisDrone2019 and UAVDT aerial image datasets, with mAP50 metrics reaching 52.5%and 33.4% respectively, which is better than other SOTA models, demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method.
